entries.reason: Registered in examples/system-process-tree.json after Phase 1 validator, publisher, process-tree and Obsidian proof.
notes: Self patrol block remains manually maintained so the patrol core is not overwritten by generic process-tree initialization.
Normal module changes update their module patrol block and regenerated runtime status; patrol-core changes require protocol confirmation.
Re-run npm.cmd run system-patrol:validate and npm.cmd run process-tree:validate after process-tree or Obsidian registration changes.
Strict required coverage is enabled; missing block/card/event or failed validation blocks current-state claims.
Source drift detection is active; source_hash mismatches block current-state claims until reviewed and refreshed.
system-patrol:enforce is the shared CI/pre-commit entry for all-module patrol verification.
system-patrol:scaffold is the preferred entry for adding patrol coverage to new process-tree modules.
Build timeline output is mandatory; construction-time state must be appended before module gate claims current status.

rules.missing_policy: Strict mode: every process-tree visible module must have a registry entry, patrol block, status card, status event and validation evidence; missing required coverage is a required failure.
rules.normal_module_change_policy: Every visible module construction, modification, migration, replacement or refactor must run the patrol-maintainer workflow or equivalent system-patrol gate before the module can be reported current.
rules.protocol_migration_policy: Schema, registry semantics, validator rules, publisher outputs, dialogue read surfaces or 3D projection contracts require explicit confirmation and full validation.
rules.publisher_policy: Publisher remains summary-only and may publish only dialogue-readable cards/events, dialogue read indexes and source-only projection refs; no raw private payloads or external actions.
rules.source_hash_policy: Every required patrol block stores a content hash derived from non-runtime evidence.source_refs; source drift is a required failure until the affected patrol baseline is reviewed and refreshed.
rules.enforcement_entry_policy: Future visible module work must use system-patrol:scaffold for new modules when needed and system-patrol:maintain or system-patrol:enforce before current-state claims; CI/pre-commit entries call the same enforcement workflow.
rules.build_timeline_policy: Every required visible module has an append-only module_build_timeline_event.v1 output under runtime/module-build-timelines; module gate blocks current-state claims when the timeline is missing, invalid, stale, or not matched to the module.
